steps.title: Welcome to DJ Engine
steps.body:
This tutorial walks you through making your first game.

You'll learn where everything lives in the editor, place entities on a scene, write dialogue in the story graph, and preview your game.

Click Next to begin.

steps.title: The Menu Bar
steps.body:
This is the menu bar.

- File: Save and load projects
- View selector: Switch between Level Editor and Story Graph
- Play button: Launch the runtime preview

Click Next to continue.

steps.title: The Browser Panel
steps.body:
The left panel has four tabs:

- Palette: Select objects to paint on the grid
- Hierarchy: Scene entity tree
- Files: Browse project assets
- Docs: Custom data documents

Click Next to continue.

steps.title: Place an Entity
steps.body:
Let's place something on the scene!

1. Click the Palette tab in the left panel
2. Select a tile type (e.g. Terrain)
3. Click anywhere on the grid

This step completes automatically when you place an entity.

steps.title: Switch to Story Graph
steps.body:
Now let's look at the narrative editor.

Click the Story Graph view selector in the menu bar (the button that says Story Graph).

This step completes automatically when you switch views.

steps.title: Select a Story Node
steps.body:
This is the story graph editor. Each node is a narrative beat.

The starter project has a simple graph:
Start -> Dialogue -> End

Click on any node to select it.

This step completes automatically when you select a node.

steps.title: The Inspector
steps.body:
The right panel is the inspector. It shows properties of whatever you've selected.

With a story node selected, you can edit:
- Node position
- Speaker ID and dialogue text
- Target scene for End nodes

Try editing the dialogue text, then click Next.

steps.title: Run Your Game
steps.body:
You're ready to preview!

Click the Play button in the menu bar to launch the runtime preview. It opens in a separate window.

That's the basics. From here you can:
- Add more entities and scenes
- Build branching story graphs
- Import custom data documents

Click Done to close this tutorial.
